task_completion.description: Agent logged into Splunk and opened the Search & Reporting app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Web UI is visible in a browser, logged in (likely as 'admin'), and the 'Search & Reporting' app interface is active.

task_completion.description: Agent queried the 'security_logs' index for failed logins.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk search bar contains a query referencing `index="security_logs"` (or similar) along with keywords or fields filtering for failed authentication/logins.

task_completion.description: Agent formulated a search to identify users targeted by 5 or more distinct source IPs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search query includes statistical commands to count distinct IPs per user (e.g., `stats dc(src_ip) by user`) and a filtering command for 5 or more (e.g., `where count >= 5`).

task_completion.description: Agent created and scheduled an hourly alert named 'Distributed_Brute_Force_Alert'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As Alert' dialog or Alert configuration screen is visible showing the title 'Distributed_Brute_Force_Alert' and the schedule type set to run on an hourly basis (e.g., cron schedule '0 * * * *' or UI dropdown set to 'Run every hour').

task_completion.description: Agent created a dashboard named 'Botnet_Targeting_Dashboard' with a panel showing targeted users and distinct attacking IP counts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashboard titled 'Botnet_Targeting_Dashboard' is visible, containing a panel (table or chart) that displays usernames alongside a count of distinct attacking IPs.

task_completion.description: Agent added a second panel to the dashboard summarizing data using the 'iplocation' command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Botnet_Targeting_Dashboard' contains a second panel showing geographic data (e.g., a map visualization or a table with Country/City/Region columns), or the underlying SPL query for the panel is visible and includes the `iplocation` command.

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web UI to create the artifacts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Splunk web interface in a browser (clicking 'Save As', editing dashboards), rather than using terminal commands (like curl) or editing Splunk .conf files directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual log data rather than hardcoding mock results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The search results and dashboard panels display realistic, varied log data (actual IPs, timestamps, usernames) rather than static, manually typed text or mock tables.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply copy-paste the final expected XML or configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate steps of typing SPL queries, configuring alert settings, and adding dashboard panels are visible across the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The alert must be named exactly 'Distributed_Brute_Force_Alert'. The dashboard must be named exactly 'Botnet_Targeting_Dashboard'. The search must query the 'security_logs' index. The dashboard must utilize the 'iplocation' command for one of its panels. Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
